rawText:
Just as the original Gilded Age of the late nineteenth century promised the triumph of free labour after the Civil War yet delivered instead a nation of wage labourers beholden to the powerful few, today's economy -- dominated by a handful of tech billionaires who crowded the stage at a presidential inauguration -- has resurrected the very plutocratic order that the regulatory state was designed to bury.

The nineteenth-century notion of "competency," a word that once denoted a sum sufficient to support a family and secure a comfortable old age, has all but vanished from the national vocabulary, replaced by an ideology in which wealth itself serves as the measure of merit and the maximum production of riches eclipses any broader conception of public welfare.

Where railways once symbolised the volatile, boom-and-bust capitalism of the original Gilded Age -- overbuilt on borrowed capital and prone to spectacular collapse -- the digital economy now occupies a strikingly similar role, with the "magnificent seven" ruling financial markets and government tax policies allowing wealth to flow relentlessly upward.

Histories of the period, from accounts of the Haymarket bombing that rekindled fears of societal fracture to biographies of figures like John D. Rockefeller -- the son of a con artist who built a monopolistic empire through ruthlessness and guile -- reveal that the accumulation of outsized corporate power has always provoked a countervailing liberal impulse, one that is conspicuously absent today.

The muckraking journalists who once exposed the squalor of New York tenements and the horrors of Chicago's meatpacking plants, prompting reforms in housing and food safety, have no clear successors in an age when misinformation proliferates faster than investigative reporting can correct it, and when the regulatory apparatus they helped build has been hollowed out from within.

If one legacy of the original Gilded Age was the "trophy wife" -- the glamorous younger partner paired with a wealthy older man, a fixture of both fiction and the Forbes 400 -- recent data suggest that even this cultural norm is fading, as the richest American husbands no longer differ from the average population in the age gap with their spouses, a shift driven by rising female education and the social networks that form within it.

The economics of intimate life are themselves undergoing a profound transformation, with platforms like OnlyFans lowering barriers to entry into sex work, digital technology eroding the stigma around it, and economists -- who long blushed at the subject -- now employing creative methods, from capture-recapture surveys in Rwanda to analysis of online marketplace reviews, to measure an industry that generates nearly $100 billion in pornography revenues alone.

What distinguishes this new gilded era from the old is not merely the scale of inequality but the absence of the anti-monopoly politics that once permeated every major party, advocating for an even playing field and a broad public welfare -- a tradition that has been supplanted by the belief that the success of the republic should be measured in low consumer prices and a rising GDP rather than in the economic independence of its citizens.

The nineteenth-century plutocracy, for all its corruption and excess, never produced the strongman that the Founding Fathers feared would endanger the republic, whereas today's concentration of wealth and political power has enabled precisely such a figure to emerge, blurring the line between self-dealing and governance in ways that would have alarmed even the most cynical robber barons.

History suggests that it was politics, not economics, that ultimately brought the original Gilded Age to an end -- through the Progressive Era reforms and eventually the New Deal -- and whether this second iteration will likewise yield to a corrective movement depends on whether citizens, confronted with the rot beneath the gilding, can summon the political will that their predecessors, after decades of struggle, eventually found.

overview.title: The New Gilded Age: Inequality, Plutocrats, and Shifting Norms

sentences.original: Just as the original Gilded Age of the late nineteenth century promised the triumph of free labour after the Civil War yet delivered instead a nation of wage labourers beholden to the powerful few, today's economy -- dominated by a handful of tech billionaires who crowded the stage at a presidential inauguration -- has resurrected the very plutocratic order that the regulatory state was designed to bury.
sentences.translation: 19세기 후반의 도금 시대가 남북전쟁 이후 자유 노동의 승리를 약속했지만 실제로는 소수의 권력자에게 종속된 임금 노동자들의 나라를 만들어 냈듯이, 오늘날의 경제도 — 대통령 취임식 무대에 올라선 소수의 기술 억만장자가 지배하는 — 규제 국가가 묻어 버리려 했던 바로 그 금권 체제를 부활시켰다.

grammarElements.text: Just as the original Gilded Age ... delivered instead a nation of wage labourers
grammarElements.label: Just as 비교 부사절

grammarElements.text: that the regulatory state was designed to bury
grammarElements.label: 관계절

vocabulary.example: Workers felt beholden to the company that controlled their housing.
Critics warn of a plutocratic drift in modern democracies.

expressions.usage: 과거와 현재의 유사성을 구조적으로 대비할 때 사용하는 격식 표현

patterns.explanation: 과거 사례와 현재 상황을 나란히 놓아 역사적 반복을 강조하는 패턴
examples: Just as the Roman Republic fell to internal division, modern democracies face similar threats.
Just as railways once transformed commerce, digital platforms now reshape entire industries.

drillQuestions.question: Today's economy has resurrected the very plutocratic order that the regulatory state was designed to ______.

sentences.notes: Just as 구조가 도금 시대와 현대를 역사적으로 병치시키며 글 전체의 핵심 논제를 세운다.

koreanAlignment.en: the original Gilded Age of the late nineteenth century
a nation of wage labourers beholden to the powerful few
koreanAlignment.ko: 소수의 권력자에게 종속된 임금 노동자들의 나라

koreanAlignment.en: has resurrected the very plutocratic order
the regulatory state was designed to bury

pronunciationNotes: plutocratic: /ˌpluːtəˈkrætɪk/ 강세가 세 번째 음절에 온다

sentences.original: The nineteenth-century notion of "competency," a word that once denoted a sum sufficient to support a family and secure a comfortable old age, has all but vanished from the national vocabulary, replaced by an ideology in which wealth itself serves as the measure of merit and the maximum production of riches eclipses any broader conception of public welfare.
sentences.translation: 19세기에 '적정 자산'이라는 개념 — 가족을 부양하고 편안한 노후를 보장하기에 충분한 금액을 뜻하던 말 — 은 국가적 어휘에서 거의 사라졌고, 부 자체가 능력의 척도가 되고 부의 극대화가 공공복리에 대한 모든 폭넓은 관념을 가리는 이념으로 대체되었다.

grammarElements.text: a word that once denoted a sum sufficient to support a family
grammarElements.label: 동격 설명구

vocabulary.example: Short-term profits eclipsed any concern for long-term sustainability.

expressions.usage: 어떤 것이 거의 소멸했음을 강조할 때 쓰는 관용 표현

patterns.pattern: [Concept] has all but vanished, replaced by [new reality]
patterns.explanation: 과거의 가치가 사라지고 새로운 이념으로 대체되었음을 표현하는 패턴
examples: The ideal of lifetime employment has all but vanished, replaced by gig contracts.
Civic duty has all but vanished, replaced by consumer choice.

drillQuestions.question: The nineteenth-century notion of "competency" has all but ______ from the national vocabulary.

sentences.notes: all but vanished 표현이 개념의 소멸을 강조하며, 동격 구문이 '적정 자산'의 원래 뜻을 복원해 준다.

koreanAlignment.en: The nineteenth-century notion of "competency"
has all but vanished from the national vocabulary
wealth itself serves as the measure of merit
eclipses any broader conception of public welfare
koreanAlignment.ko: 공공복리에 대한 모든 폭넓은 관념을 가린다

sentences.original: Where railways once symbolised the volatile, boom-and-bust capitalism of the original Gilded Age -- overbuilt on borrowed capital and prone to spectacular collapse -- the digital economy now occupies a strikingly similar role, with the "magnificent seven" ruling financial markets and government tax policies allowing wealth to flow relentlessly upward.
sentences.translation: 과거 철도가 도금 시대의 변동성 크고 호황과 불황을 오가는 자본주의를 상징했듯이 — 빌린 자본으로 과잉 건설되어 극적인 붕괴를 맞기 쉬웠던 — 오늘날 디지털 경제는 놀라울 만큼 비슷한 역할을 차지하고 있으며, '매그니피센트 세븐'이 금융시장을 지배하고 정부 조세 정책이 부가 끊임없이 위로 흘러가도록 허용하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: with the "magnificent seven" ruling financial markets
grammarElements.label: with + 분사 구문 (부대상황)

vocabulary.example: Volatile markets make long-term planning difficult for investors.

expressions.usage: 경기 순환의 극단적 진폭을 묘사할 때 사용

patterns.explanation: 과거와 현재의 유사 현상을 병치하여 역사적 반복을 보여주는 패턴
examples: Where factories once defined urban life, tech campuses now do.
Where newspapers once shaped opinion, social media now fills that role.

drillQuestions.question: The digital economy now occupies a strikingly ______ role.

sentences.notes: Where ... once / now 구조로 철도와 디지털 경제의 역사적 평행을 선명하게 그린다.

koreanAlignment.en: Where railways once symbolised
volatile, boom-and-bust capitalism
koreanAlignment.ko: 변동성 크고 호황과 불황을 오가는 자본주의

koreanAlignment.en: the digital economy now occupies a strikingly similar role
koreanAlignment.ko: 오늘날 디지털 경제는 놀라울 만큼 비슷한 역할을 차지하고 있다

sentences.original: Histories of the period, from accounts of the Haymarket bombing that rekindled fears of societal fracture to biographies of figures like John D. Rockefeller -- the son of a con artist who built a monopolistic empire through ruthlessness and guile -- reveal that the accumulation of outsized corporate power has always provoked a countervailing liberal impulse, one that is conspicuously absent today.
sentences.translation: 이 시대에 대한 역사서들은 — 사회 분열에 대한 공포를 다시 불러일으킨 헤이마켓 폭탄 사건의 기록에서부터, 사기꾼의 아들로 태어나 무자비함과 간계로 독점 제국을 세운 존 D. 록펠러 같은 인물의 전기에 이르기까지 — 거대 기업 권력의 축적이 늘 이에 대항하는 자유주의적 반작용을 불러왔음을 보여 주지만, 그러한 반작용은 오늘날 눈에 띄게 부재하다.

grammarElements.text: from accounts of ... to biographies of ...
grammarElements.label: from A to B 범위 수식구

grammarElements.text: that the accumulation of outsized corporate power has always provoked a countervailing liberal impulse
grammarElements.label: that 명사절 (목적어)

vocabulary.example: Every concentration of power eventually meets a countervailing force.
He advanced through a combination of talent and guile.

expressions.usage: 마땅히 있어야 할 것이 없음을 강조할 때 쓰는 격식 표현

patterns.pattern: [Evidence] reveal(s) that [pattern], one that is [current status]
patterns.explanation: 역사적 증거가 반복 패턴을 드러내고 현재 상태를 덧붙이는 구조
examples: Studies reveal that inequality always provokes backlash, one that is building slowly.
Records reveal that markets always correct, one that is overdue now.

drillQuestions.question: The accumulation of outsized corporate power has always provoked a ______ liberal impulse.

sentences.notes: from A to B 구조와 대시(—) 삽입절이 겹치며 문장이 길어지지만, 핵심은 reveal that 절에 있다.

koreanAlignment.en: Histories of the period
a countervailing liberal impulse
conspicuously absent today

pronunciationNotes: countervailing: /ˌkaʊntərˈveɪlɪŋ/ 두 번째 요소 vail에 강세

sentences.original: The muckraking journalists who once exposed the squalor of New York tenements and the horrors of Chicago's meatpacking plants, prompting reforms in housing and food safety, have no clear successors in an age when misinformation proliferates faster than investigative reporting can correct it, and when the regulatory apparatus they helped build has been hollowed out from within.
sentences.translation: 뉴욕 빈민가의 비참한 환경과 시카고 육가공 공장의 참상을 폭로하여 주거와 식품 안전 개혁을 촉발했던 폭로 저널리스트들은, 허위 정보가 탐사 보도가 바로잡을 수 있는 속도보다 빠르게 퍼지고 그들이 세우는 데 기여한 규제 체계가 내부에서부터 무너져 내린 시대에 뚜렷한 후계자가 없다.

grammarElements.text: who once exposed the squalor ... prompting reforms
grammarElements.label: 관계절 + 분사구문

grammarElements.text: in an age when misinformation proliferates faster than ...
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 + when 관계절

vocabulary.example: Reporters documented the squalor of overcrowded urban housing.

expressions.usage: 겉은 유지되지만 실질이 사라졌음을 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Past actors] who once [achievement] have no clear successors in an age when [new obstacle]
patterns.explanation: 과거의 역할을 수행하던 이들이 현재는 대체되지 않았음을 보여주는 패턴
examples: The public intellectuals who once shaped debate have no clear successors in an age of algorithmic feeds.
The labor unions who once protected workers have no clear successors in an age of gig employment.

drillQuestions.question: The muckraking journalists have no clear ______ in an age when misinformation proliferates.

sentences.notes: 주어에서 동사까지의 거리가 길어 읽기 어렵지만, 핵심은 have no clear successors라는 간결한 판단이다.

koreanAlignment.en: The muckraking journalists
the squalor of New York tenements
misinformation proliferates faster than investigative reporting can correct it
koreanAlignment.ko: 허위 정보가 탐사 보도가 바로잡을 수 있는 속도보다 빠르게 퍼진다

koreanAlignment.en: hollowed out from within

sentences.original: If one legacy of the original Gilded Age was the "trophy wife" -- the glamorous younger partner paired with a wealthy older man, a fixture of both fiction and the Forbes 400 -- recent data suggest that even this cultural norm is fading, as the richest American husbands no longer differ from the average population in the age gap with their spouses, a shift driven by rising female education and the social networks that form within it.
sentences.translation: 도금 시대의 유산 중 하나가 '트로피 와이프' — 부유한 연상 남성과 짝지어진 화려한 젊은 배우자, 소설과 포브스 400 목록 양쪽에 등장하는 존재 — 였다면, 최근 데이터는 이 문화적 규범마저 사라지고 있음을 보여 준다. 미국에서 가장 부유한 남편들이 배우자와의 나이 차이에서 더 이상 일반 인구와 다르지 않으며, 이는 여성 교육 수준의 향상과 그 안에서 형성되는 사회적 관계망이 이끈 변화이다.

grammarElements.text: If one legacy of the original Gilded Age was the "trophy wife"
grammarElements.label: If 조건절 (가정적 전제)

grammarElements.text: that even this cultural norm is fading
grammarElements.label: that 명사절

grammarElements.text: a shift driven by rising female education
grammarElements.label: 동격 설명구

vocabulary.example: The annual charity gala became a fixture of the social calendar.

expressions.usage: 이전에 존재하던 차이가 사라졌음을 나타낼 때 사용

patterns.pattern: If one legacy of [era] was [norm], recent data suggest that [change]
patterns.explanation: 역사적 유산을 전제로 놓고 최근 데이터로 변화를 보여주는 패턴
examples: If one legacy of industrialisation was the nuclear family, recent data suggest that it is dissolving.
If one legacy of the Cold War was military spending, recent data suggest that priorities have shifted.

drillQuestions.question: Recent data suggest that even this cultural norm is ______.

sentences.notes: If ... was 구조가 과거의 고정관념을 전제로 제시한 뒤 데이터로 반전시키는 기법이다.

koreanAlignment.en: the glamorous younger partner paired with a wealthy older man
koreanAlignment.ko: 부유한 연상 남성과 짝지어진 화려한 젊은 배우자

koreanAlignment.en: no longer differ from the average population
koreanAlignment.ko: 더 이상 일반 인구와 다르지 않다

koreanAlignment.en: rising female education

sentences.original: The economics of intimate life are themselves undergoing a profound transformation, with platforms like OnlyFans lowering barriers to entry into sex work, digital technology eroding the stigma around it, and economists -- who long blushed at the subject -- now employing creative methods, from capture-recapture surveys in Rwanda to analysis of online marketplace reviews, to measure an industry that generates nearly $100 billion in pornography revenues alone.
sentences.translation: 사적인 삶의 경제학 자체도 심대한 변환을 겪고 있다. 온리팬스 같은 플랫폼이 성 산업 진입 장벽을 낮추고, 디지털 기술이 그에 대한 낙인을 허물며, 오랫동안 이 주제에 얼굴을 붉히던 경제학자들이 이제는 — 르완다에서의 포획-재포획 조사부터 온라인 마켓플레이스 리뷰 분석에 이르기까지 — 창의적 방법을 동원하여 포르노 수익만으로도 거의 1,000억 달러를 창출하는 산업을 측정하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: with platforms ... lowering / technology eroding / economists ... employing
grammarElements.label: with + 분사 3중 병렬 (부대상황)

vocabulary.example: Digital anonymity has helped erode the stigma around mental health treatment.

expressions.usage: 어떤 산업이나 시장에 새로 들어가기 어렵게 만드는 요소를 가리킬 때 쓰는 경제학 용어

patterns.pattern: [Domain] is undergoing a profound transformation, with [factor 1], [factor 2], and [factor 3]
patterns.explanation: 하나의 영역이 여러 동시적 요인에 의해 변화하고 있음을 보여주는 패턴
examples: Higher education is undergoing a profound transformation, with costs rising, enrollment falling, and AI reshaping instruction.
Retail is undergoing a profound transformation, with malls closing, delivery expanding, and data driving personalization.

drillQuestions.question: Digital technology is eroding the ______ around sex work.

sentences.notes: with + 분사 세 개의 병렬 구조가 변화의 동시성과 다면성을 한 문장으로 압축한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The economics of intimate life
lowering barriers to entry
eroding the stigma around it
nearly $100 billion in pornography revenues alone

sentences.original: What distinguishes this new gilded era from the old is not merely the scale of inequality but the absence of the anti-monopoly politics that once permeated every major party, advocating for an even playing field and a broad public welfare -- a tradition that has been supplanted by the belief that the success of the republic should be measured in low consumer prices and a rising GDP rather than in the economic independence of its citizens.
sentences.translation: 이 새로운 도금 시대를 과거와 구별 짓는 것은 불평등의 규모만이 아니라, 한때 모든 주요 정당에 스며들어 공정한 경쟁의 장과 폭넓은 공공복리를 옹호하던 반독점 정치의 부재이다 — 이 전통은 공화국의 성공이 시민의 경제적 독립이 아닌 낮은 소비자 물가와 상승하는 GDP로 측정되어야 한다는 믿음에 의해 대체되었다.

grammarElements.text: What distinguishes this new gilded era from the old
grammarElements.label: What 명사절 주어

grammarElements.text: not merely the scale of inequality but the absence of the anti-monopoly politics
grammarElements.label: not merely A but B 보어

grammarElements.text: a tradition that has been supplanted by the belief that ...
grammarElements.label: 동격 설명절

vocabulary.example: Old customs were supplanted by market-driven norms.
A sense of urgency permeated every department.

patterns.pattern: What distinguishes [A] from [B] is not merely [X] but [Y]
patterns.explanation: 두 대상의 결정적 차이를 밝히면서 흔한 설명을 넘어서는 핵심을 제시하는 패턴
examples: What distinguishes this crisis from past ones is not merely its severity but its speed.
What distinguishes great leaders from good ones is not merely vision but execution.

drillQuestions.question: What distinguishes this new gilded era from the old is not merely the scale of inequality but the ______ of anti-monopoly politics.

sentences.notes: not merely A but B 구조로 단순한 수치적 불평등이 아닌 정치적 대응의 부재를 핵심 차이로 제시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: What distinguishes this new gilded era from the old
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 새로운 도금 시대를 과거와 구별 짓는 것

koreanAlignment.en: the absence of the anti-monopoly politics
an even playing field
the economic independence of its citizens

pronunciationNotes: supplanted: /səˈplæntɪd/ 두 번째 음절 plant에 강세

sentences.original: The nineteenth-century plutocracy, for all its corruption and excess, never produced the strongman that the Founding Fathers feared would endanger the republic, whereas today's concentration of wealth and political power has enabled precisely such a figure to emerge, blurring the line between self-dealing and governance in ways that would have alarmed even the most cynical robber barons.
sentences.translation: 19세기 금권 정치는 온갖 부패와 과잉에도 불구하고 건국의 아버지들이 공화국을 위태롭게 할 것이라 두려워한 권력자를 결코 만들어 내지 못했지만, 오늘날 부와 정치 권력의 집중은 바로 그러한 인물의 등장을 가능하게 했으며, 가장 냉소적인 강도 귀족들마저 경악시켰을 방식으로 사익 추구와 통치 사이의 경계를 흐리고 있다.

grammarElements.text: for all its corruption and excess
grammarElements.label: 양보 전치사구 (for all = ~에도 불구하고)

grammarElements.text: whereas today's concentration ... has enabled precisely such a figure to emerge
grammarElements.label: whereas 대조절

vocabulary.example: Regulators are investigating the CEO for alleged self-dealing.

expressions.phrase: for all its corruption and excess
expressions.usage: 양보를 표현하는 격식적 전치사구, for all = despite

patterns.pattern: [Past era], for all its [flaws], never [worst outcome], whereas [present] has enabled [that outcome]
patterns.explanation: 과거조차 도달하지 못한 나쁜 결과에 현재가 도달했음을 대비하는 패턴
examples: The old regime, for all its inefficiency, never collapsed so fast, whereas the new system has already shown cracks.
Cold War rivalry, for all its danger, never triggered nuclear war, whereas today's instabilities have raised that risk.

drillQuestions.question: Today's concentration of wealth has enabled precisely such a figure to ______.

sentences.notes: for all ... never ... whereas 구조가 과거 금권정치보다 현재가 더 위험하다는 역설적 논점을 만든다.

koreanAlignment.en: for all its corruption and excess
the strongman that the Founding Fathers feared
blurring the line between self-dealing and governance
koreanAlignment.ko: 사익 추구와 통치 사이의 경계를 흐리고 있다

sentences.original: History suggests that it was politics, not economics, that ultimately brought the original Gilded Age to an end -- through the Progressive Era reforms and eventually the New Deal -- and whether this second iteration will likewise yield to a corrective movement depends on whether citizens, confronted with the rot beneath the gilding, can summon the political will that their predecessors, after decades of struggle, eventually found.
sentences.translation: 역사는 도금 시대를 궁극적으로 종식시킨 것이 경제가 아니라 정치였음을 보여 준다 — 진보주의 시대의 개혁과 결국에는 뉴딜을 통해서 — 그리고 이 두 번째 도금 시대도 마찬가지로 교정 운동에 자리를 내줄지는, 도금 아래의 부패와 맞닥뜨린 시민들이 선대가 수십 년의 투쟁 끝에 마침내 찾아낸 정치적 의지를 다시 불러일으킬 수 있느냐에 달려 있다.

grammarElements.text: that it was politics, not economics, that ultimately brought
grammarElements.label: 강조 구문 (it was ... that)

grammarElements.text: whether this second iteration will likewise yield to a corrective movement
grammarElements.label: whether 명사절

grammarElements.text: depends on whether citizens ... can summon the political will
grammarElements.label: depends on 구조

vocabulary.example: The country must summon the courage to face its structural problems.

expressions.usage: 기존 체제가 개혁적 힘에 결국 굴복하는 상황을 표현

patterns.pattern: History suggests that it was [X], not [Y], that [result]
patterns.explanation: 역사적 교훈을 강조 구문으로 전달하며 통념을 반박하는 패턴
examples: History suggests that it was leadership, not resources, that determined the outcome.
History suggests that it was institutions, not individuals, that sustained democracy.

drillQuestions.question: History suggests that it was politics, not economics, that ultimately ______ the original Gilded Age to an end.

sentences.notes: it was ... that 강조 구문과 whether ... depends on whether 이중 구조로 마무리하며 독자에게 질문을 던진다.

koreanAlignment.en: it was politics, not economics
the rot beneath the gilding
summon the political will
after decades of struggle, eventually found
koreanAlignment.ko: 수십 년의 투쟁 끝에 마침내 찾아낸
